author: Dr. Robert Kim
content:
Telephone encounter with patient Robert Morrison regarding urological symptoms and medication refill.

REASON FOR CALL: Patient calling to request refill of tamsulosin 0.4mg. Also reports mild increase in lower extremity swelling and wants to know if tamsulosin could be contributing.

MEDICATION REFILL:
- Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily — sent to CVS pharmacy (90-day supply). Patient has been on this medication since 2016 for BPH with good symptom control.

SYMPTOM REVIEW:
- BPH symptoms: Patient reports stable urinary symptoms. Stream adequate, no significant hesitancy. Nocturia 2-3 times per night (baseline for him given diuretic use and BPH). No dysuria, no hematuria. IPSS score not formally assessed today but patient describes symptoms as "manageable."
- Leg swelling concern: Patient notes mild bilateral ankle edema, worse at end of day. This is more likely related to his heart failure and diuretic management than tamsulosin. Tamsulosin can occasionally cause peripheral edema but this has been stable on his current dose for years. Advised him to monitor daily weights, elevate legs when sitting, and contact his PCP or cardiologist if swelling worsens, weight increases >3 lbs in a day or >5 lbs in a week, or if he develops increased shortness of breath.

REVIEWED CHART: Noted patient has upcoming cardiology appointment. Deferred edema management to Dr. Wilson (cardiology) and Dr. Adams (PCP) as this is more likely cardiac/volume-related.

ADDITIONAL: Patient asked about PSA screening — last PSA was 3.2 ng/mL (June 2024), which was stable from prior. Next PSA due at annual urology visit. Reminded him his appointment is scheduled for July 2025.

PLAN:
1. Tamsulosin refill sent to pharmacy.
2. Leg swelling — monitor weights, follow up with PCP/cardiology.
3. Next urology visit July 2025 — will recheck PSA, reassess BPH symptoms.
4. If urinary symptoms worsen (inability to void, significant hematuria, fever), proceed to ED.

Total call duration: 11 minutes.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Robert Kim
Date: 2/1/2025
